# kutl

> kutl is a sync protocol for teams of humans and AI agents: it keeps a team
> coordinated on shared, living documentation. Documents are plain files on
> disk; concurrent text edits merge via CRDTs instead of forking into conflict
> copies, and the relay is self-hostable (open source). Name collisions and
> non-mergeable changes are preserved as conflict copies rather than silently
> dropped. kutlhub (https://kutlhub.com) is the hosted offering with shared
> spaces and a web editor.

## Docs

- [Getting started](https://kutl.io/docs): install, join a space, sync a folder
- [Concepts](https://kutl.io/docs/concepts): spaces, documents, relays, merge model
- [CLI reference](https://kutl.io/docs/cli): commands, flags, environment variables
- [Self-hosting a relay](https://kutl.io/docs/relay): Docker/binary relay setup
- [Conflict behavior](https://kutl.io/docs/conflicts): concurrent edit/rename/delete semantics
- [Troubleshooting](https://kutl.io/docs/troubleshooting): sync and connectivity diagnosis
